---
title: "restaurant"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
library(osmdata)

# Set a different Overpass server (this one is usually stable)
set_overpass_url("https://overpass-api.de/api/interpreter")

get_restaurants_by_city <- function(city) {
  opq(city) %>%
    add_osm_feature(key = "amenity", value = "restaurant") %>%
    osmdata_sf()
}

restaurants_baltimore <- get_restaurants_by_city("Baltimore")
```

```{r}
restaurants_baltimore$osm_points %>%
  dplyr::select(name, geometry) %>%
  head()
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Grab just the points layer
bmore_restaurants <- restaurants_baltimore$osm_points

# See what columns are available
colnames(bmore_restaurants)
```

```{r}
# Filter down to the good stuff
bmore_restaurants_clean <- bmore_restaurants %>%
  select(name, cuisine, `addr:street`)

# View the first few
head(bmore_restaurants_clean)
```

```{r}
bmore_restaurants_clean2 <- bmore_restaurants_clean %>%
  filter(!(is.na(name) & is.na(cuisine) & is.na(`addr:street`)))
write.csv(bmore_restaurants_clean2,"~/Downloads/restaurant.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
montgomery_geom <- md_counties %>%
  filter(COUNTY == "Montgomery") %>%
  st_geometry()
```

```{r}
get_restaurants_by_geom <- function(county_name, county_geom) {
  # Your API or web scraping function here
}
restaurants_montgomery <- get_restaurants_by_geom("Montgomery", montgomery_geom)

```
